title: Jewish Museum Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **The Jewish Museum Berlin core exhibition is free.** Reserve a free timed slot online if you want the calmest entry, but walk-in tickets are also available at the desk.
**Regular hours are Tuesday to Sunday, 10:00 to 18:00, with last admission at 17:00.** It is normally closed on Mondays and on a small number of additional dates.
**Allow 2.5 to 3 hours for a first visit.** Ninety minutes works only as an architecture-and-highlights route; four hours gives you room for the JMB app, closer reading and a proper pause.

**Follow the building in order:** enter through the Old Building, clear security, walk the three underground axes, then visit the Garden of Exile and Holocaust Tower before moving into the core exhibition.

**This is not only a Holocaust museum.** The lower architecture confronts exile, persecution and the Shoah, while the 3,500-square-metre core exhibition covers about 1,700 years of Jewish life in Germany from the Middle Ages to today.

**Bring a small bag and expect security.** Large bags, backpacks, umbrellas and heavy coats go to lockers or the cloakroom. Most public spaces are step-free, but the narrow Garden of Exile route is not wheelchair accessible.
